content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to reverse a given string using pointers.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single string SSS - The string which needs to be reversed.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the reversed string.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of input contains a single string S (1 ≤ |S| ≤ 100) consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: Print the reversed string on a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: A single‑character string remains unchanged when reversed.

solution.approach: Use two pointers that start at the beginning and end of the string. While the left pointer is before the right pointer, swap the characters at these positions and move the pointers towards each other. This reverses the string in‑place in linear time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) where L is the length of the string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the reversal is done in‑place).

code.Python3:
import sys
s=sys.stdin.readline().strip()
if s:
    sys.stdout.write(s[::-1])
